preamble: The correspondence between the voiceless velar fricative [x] and the voiceless bilabial stop [p] is a documented phonetic shift in several language families, often mediated by labiovelar segments or through complex fortition paths.

examples.note: Widespread shift where historical labiovelar fricatives or stops shifted to bilabial stops in the P-Celtic branch (e.g., Welsh).
Historical paths where back segments through intermediate rounded stages fortified into bilabial stops.
